title: Migration
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Migration
text:
Venezuela After Maduro: Q&A with RAND Experts
Many questions remain one month after the dramatic U.S. operation that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. How will sudden change reshape Venezuela's future? What are the regional impacts? And how might the U.S.-Venezuela relationship evolve?
Small Boats and Shifting Threats: Britain Can't Keep Fighting Yesterday's Battle in the Channel
Unless the UK and its European partners update their approach to Channel crossings in 2026, they risk merely managing arrivals rather than disrupting the criminal systems that drive them.
Finding International Rockstar Researchers: Identifying Predictors of Success Following an International Move
After relocating internationally, some researchers thrive in their new environments, producing discoveries at rates higher than before their move. This report aims to identify predictors of successful outcomes after an international move.
Parallels and Discrepancies Between Non-Native Species Introductions and Human Migration
We compare biological invasions with human migration, integrating historical, socio-political, and ethical perspectives to identify context-specific approaches in policymaking and governance to address the challenges and opportunities of human migration and harm from biological invasions.
Forced Labor in Global Supply Chains: Trade Enforcement Impacts and Opportunities
This report discusses methods of evaluating the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's impact on forced labor globally, presents findings on the department’s efforts, and provides recommendations for strengthening enforcement.
Combating Forced Labor in Global Supply Chains: Is U.S. Trade Enforcement Making a Difference, and Can It Do More?
U.S. trade enforcement is combating the use of forced labor. It can also do more. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security's and businesses' efforts have decreased the direct import of goods from the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region.
Understanding Climate Migration
Climate migration is complex, with many factors influencing why and where people move. While some may relocate due to climate change, most stay put. Effective policies are needed to help people adapt, relocate safely if necessary, and integrate into new communities.
Emerging Technologies in the Humanitarian Sector: Technology Deep Dive Series
RAND Europe, in partnership with Athena Infonomics and glass.ai, conducted a study exploring the adoption and use of emerging technologies in the humanitarian sector out to 2030, as well as associated risks, challenges and opportunities.
Opportunities for supporting humanitarians: Technology guidance
RAND Europe, in partnership with Athena Infonomics and glass.ai, conducted a study exploring the adoption and use of emerging technologies in the humanitarian sector out to 2030, as well as associated risks, challenges and opportunities.
Opportunities for supporting humanitarians: Technology foresight concepts
RAND Europe, in partnership with Athena Infonomics and glass.ai, conducted a study exploring the adoption and use of emerging technologies in the humanitarian sector out to 2030, as well as associated risks, challenges and opportunities.
